_comment: Runtime configuration for ai-code-cognitive-stress. Edit these defaults to change behaviour without touching code.
_comment_work_window: The work window is inferred per-user from interaction timestamps by default (p10–p90 of your daytime message hours, requiring at least 5 distinct weekdays of data). To PIN a manual window instead, add a 'work_window' block with 'start' and 'end' (HH:MM, local time) and it will override inference. Example: {"start": "09:00", "end": "19:00"}.

codl._comment: Engagement weighting for CODL. A session counts at full weight (1.0) only while you're actively driving it — within foreground_grace_minutes of one of YOUR messages. The rest of the time it is alive but 'cooking' in the background and counts at background_weight. Research basis: a background locus is the 'out-of-the-loop' supervisory regime, where monitoring is effortful and stressful (Warm et al. 2008; Endsley & Kiris 1995; Parasuraman & Manzey 2010) — discounted but never free. The magnitude (0.25) is borrowed from the prospective-memory monitoring cost of ~15-20% of ongoing-task capacity (Smith 2003; Masicampo & Baumeister 2011), at the conservative top of that range.

resumption._comment: Scoring for the Closure Deficit, now measured as RESUMPTION LOAD — loops you couldn't finish in one sitting and had to pick back up. A 'resume' is a true-idle gap in a session's timeline (no user OR agent event) of at least threshold_minutes, OR a same-session pickup on a later calendar day. Each resume's severity = min(1, gap / full_decay_minutes), so cost rises with how long the loop was parked then saturates (a 6h park is no colder than a 2h one once context is fully gone). The day's axis value = min(1, sum(severity) / daily_ceiling), in [0,1]; 0 means every loop closed in one sitting. No git needed — scored on every active day. Research basis: resumption cost rises with interruption DURATION (Monk, Trafton & Boehm-Davis 2008); a suspended goal's activation decays over the gap and must be reconstructed (Altmann & Trafton 2002); resuming an interrupted programming task incurs a context-reconstruction tax (Parnin & Rugaber 2011); closure/disengagement is a recovery resource (Sonnentag & Fritz 2007); the tendency to resume interrupted tasks replicates while the older memory-persistence claim does not (Ghibellini & Meier 2025). daily_ceiling ~4 is a loose Cowan (2001) anchor. Modeling priors, calibratable, NOT validated against felt load. CAVEAT: lab studies measured short (sub-minute) gaps; multi-hour/cross-day gaps extrapolate beyond that regime (Parnin & Rugaber is the field bridge).

scoring._comment: Composite-scoring scales and weights. Each axis is mapped to [0,1] by dividing by its ceiling (codl_avg / codl_ceiling, interruption_rate / interruption_ceiling; closure_deficit/resumption load is already 0..1), then blended with 'weights' (codl, interruption, closure; normalized by their sum). Defaults are the v1 literature / null-hypothesis values: codl_ceiling=5 (one above Cowan's ~4 capacity limit), interruption_ceiling=10/hour, equal weights. These can be replaced with values suggested by population calibration (run: aicogstress --calibrate <export files>) — review the calibration report first. Calibrated weights are an unsupervised heuristic, NOT validated against felt load.
